title: Sustainability and Environmental, Social, and Governance Africa Conference and Expo 2026: Africa’s Premier Platform for Sustainable Business, Innovation, and Leadership
summary: Join Africa’s Leading Sustainability and Environmental, Social, and Governance Event in Johannesburg, South Africa The Sustainability and Environmental, Social, and Governance (ESG) Africa Conference and Expo 2026 is set to take place from 30 September to 1 October 2026 at the prestigious Sandton Convention Centre, Johannesburg, South Africa . Recognized as Africa’s largest and most influential executive platform focused on sustainability and ESG, the conference brings together business leaders, policymakers, investors, innovators, academics, and sustainability practitioners to explore the future of sustainable business across the continent. As sustainability continues to evolve from a compliance-driven requirement into a core business strategy, organizations are increasingly being challenged to demonstrate how sustainability contributes to profitability, resilience, capital access, and long-term value creation. The Sustainability and ESG Africa Conference and Expo 2026 aims to provide practical answers to these challenges while fostering collaboration and innovation among stakeholders shaping Africa’s sustainable future. Why the Sustainability and ESG Africa Conference and Expo 2026 Matters The sustainability landscape has undergone a significant transformation in recent years. Organizations are no longer judged solely on their sustainability reports or environmental commitments. Instead, investors, customers, regulators, and stakeholders expect businesses to demonstrate measurable outcomes and real economic value from their sustainability initiatives. The conference addresses a critical question facing organizations today: Does sustainability make business sense? Through expert-led discussions, case studies, masterclasses, exhibitions, and networking opportunities, participants will gain valuable insights into how sustainability can drive business growth, operational resilience, and long-term competitiveness. Event Details Conference Dates 30 September – 1 October 2026 Venue Sandton Convention Centre Sandton, Johannesburg, South Africa Location Address 161 Maude Street Sandown, Sandton, 2196 Johannesburg, South Africa What Makes This Conference Unique? The Sustainability and ESG Africa Conference and Expo has established itself as the continent’s most trusted sustainability gathering. It offers a unique platform where sustainability is discussed not only as a social responsibility issue but as a strategic business imperative. Key Highlights Include: 1. Africa’s Premier ESG Platform The conference is widely regarded as Africa’s largest and longest-running sustainability and ESG event. It serves as a meeting point for influential leaders driving sustainability initiatives across various industries. 2. Access to Influential Leaders Participants gain access to more than 100 sustainability leaders, executives, policymakers, and experts who are actively shaping the future of responsible business and sustainable development across Africa. 3. Year-Round Sustainability Community The conference extends beyond the two-day event through ongoing engagement opportunities, fostering continuous collaboration, networking, and learning throughout the year. 4. Sustainability Solutions Exhibition The expo features dedicated exhibition spaces showcasing innovative sustainability products, services, technologies, and business solutions designed to address real-world challenges. 5. Recognition of Excellence The event hosts prestigious award programs that celebrate sustainability leadership, innovation, and impactful contributions to ESG advancement across Africa. These include: Beyond Awards YES Awards Student Innovation Awards 6. Practical Masterclasses Attendees can participate in hands-on learning experiences designed to help organizations move from sustainability strategy to practical implementation. 7. Executive-Level Networking The CEO Breakfast provides a unique opportunity for C-suite executives and senior decision-makers to engage in strategic discussions on the future of sustainability. 8. Real Impact Stories Industry leaders share practical experiences, measurable outcomes, lessons learned, and proven approaches to implementing sustainability initiatives successfully. 9. Africa-Centric Solutions Unlike many global sustainability events, this conference is specifically designed to address Africa’s unique opportunities, challenges, and development priorities while maintaining global relevance. Key Benefits of Attending Participants can expect numerous benefits, including: Professional Development Learn from leading sustainability experts. Gain practical knowledge on ESG implementation. Stay informed about emerging sustainability trends. Networking Opportunities Connect with industry leaders. Build relationships with investors and policymakers. Collaborate with sustainability practitioners across sectors. Business Growth Discover sustainability solutions that improve business performance. Explore new partnership opportunities. Identify strategies for accessing sustainable finance. Innovation Exposure Learn about cutting-edge sustainability technologies. Explore innovative business models. Engage with emerging sustainability startups and innovators. Strategic Insights Understand evolving ESG regulations and expectations. Learn how sustainability can enhance organizational resilience. Discover strategies for long-term value creation. Additional Learning Opportunities Throughout the Year Beyond the flagship conference, the Sustainability and ESG Africa platform hosts several events designed to encourage continuous engagement and knowledge sharing. Community of Practice Session: Climate Adaptation and Nature-Based Solutions Date: 26 May 2026 Time: 16:00 – 18:00 Format: Online (Zoom) This session focuses on: Climate adaptation strategies. Nature-based solutions. Building organizational resilience. Addressing climate-related risks. Long-term sustainability planning. Webinar: The Future of Voluntary Carbon Markets in Africa – From Potential to Value Date: 11 June 2026 Time: 15:00 – 16:30 Format: Online (Zoom) Key discussion areas include: Opportunities within Africa’s voluntary carbon market. Policy and regulatory considerations. Responsible participation frameworks. Sustainable development impacts. Strategies for capturing long-term value. What Previous Attendees Say Past participants consistently describe the Sustainability and ESG Africa Conference as a transformative experience that goes beyond traditional conference formats. Common themes highlighted by attendees include: Meaningful networking opportunities. High-quality discussions and thought leadership. Practical and actionable sustainability insights. Exposure to innovative solutions. Strong focus on African sustainability realities. Opportunities to engage with future innovators. Cross-sector collaboration and knowledge sharing. Industry leaders from organizations such as consulting firms, financial institutions, multinational corporations, technology companies, mining organizations, and sustainability-focused enterprises have praised the event for its relevance, impact, and practical value. Who Should Attend? The conference is ideal for: Corporate executives and CEOs. Sustainability and ESG managers. Investors and financial professionals. Policymakers and regulators. Entrepreneurs and business owners. Climate and environmental specialists. Supply chain professionals. Researchers and academics. Students and emerging innovators. Development practitioners. Non-governmental organizations. Consultants and advisors. Sustainability and ESG: The Future of Business in Africa As sustainability becomes increasingly central to business success, organizations must adapt to changing stakeholder expectations, investor demands, and market realities. The Sustainability and ESG Africa Conference and Expo 2026 offers an invaluable platform for learning, collaboration, and action. By bringing together diverse stakeholders from across Africa and beyond, the conference helps drive meaningful conversations, practical solutions, and measurable impact in the sustainability space. Whether you are a seasoned sustainability professional, a business executive seeking strategic insights, an investor exploring sustainable opportunities, or a student passionate about shaping a better future, this conference provides the knowledge, connections, and inspiration needed to advance sustainability and ESG goals. Deadline There is currently no application deadline specified for attendance, sponsorship, exhibition opportunities, or participation. Interested individuals and organizations are encouraged to register or secure participation opportunities as early as possible. Application and Further Information Official Website: https://esgafricaconference.com/ For inquiries: Email: hannelie@esgafricaconference.com Stay connected with the Sustainability and ESG Africa community and be part of the movement shaping the future of sustainable business across Africa. For more information about this opportunity, visit here . Discover more Global Conference Opportunities on OFY here . The post Sustainability and Environmental, Social, and Governance Africa Conference and Expo 2026: Africa’s Premier Platform for Sustainable Business, Innovation, and Leadership appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: NASA EarthRISE Developers Academy Fall 2026 Applications Now Open | Join NASA’s Next Generation of Earth Observation Innovators
summary: The National Aeronautics and Space Administration (NASA) has officially opened applications for the NASA EarthRISE Developers Academy Fall 2026 cohort, an exciting professional development and applied research opportunity designed to prepare the next generation of Earth observation and geospatial technology leaders. The EarthRISE Developers Academy offers participants the opportunity to work on real-world interdisciplinary projects using NASA Earth observation data to solve pressing global challenges. Through collaborative learning, applied research, networking, and mentorship, the Academy equips participants with practical experience in Earth science applications, GIS, remote sensing, and decision-support technologies. The Fall 2026 term will run from September 14 to November 20, 2026, and applications are currently open until June 26, 2026. About NASA EarthRISE Developers Academy NASA EarthRISE Developers Academy is a workforce development initiative led by NASA Langley Research Center. Building on more than 25 years of workforce development expertise, the program immerses participants in short-term applied science projects that use NASA data and technologies to address real-world environmental and societal issues. The Academy focuses on developing innovative applied science solutions that help organizations make more effective and efficient decisions. Participants collaborate with state, local, tribal, territorial, and private-sector partners while gaining hands-on experience in using Earth observation technologies for practical applications. The program also helps bridge Earth science with the future of space exploration by preparing participants with interdisciplinary technical and professional skills relevant to the rapidly growing geospatial and Earth observation workforce. Key Program Details Application Deadline June 26, 2026 Program Dates September 14 – November 20, 2026 Program Format Virtual and agency-wide opportunities available Collaborative interdisciplinary team projects Short-term applied research experience NASA Earth Action Thematic Areas Projects within the EarthRISE Developers Academy typically focus on one or more of NASA’s Earth Action thematic priorities, including: Agriculture Disasters Ecological Conservation Energy Infrastructure Health Air Quality Water Resources Wildland Fires These thematic areas are designed to address major environmental and societal challenges through data-driven innovation and Earth observation technologies. Who Should Apply? NASA encourages applications from individuals with interdisciplinary backgrounds and varying levels of professional experience. The program welcomes: Currently enrolled students Recent graduates Early-career professionals Transitioning career professionals Applicants with experience in the following areas are particularly encouraged to apply: Earth Science Environmental Science Planetary Science Geographic Information Systems (GIS) Remote Sensing Computer Science Data Analytics Earth Observation Applications Successful participants are typically: Open-minded Collaborative Adaptable Eager to learn Interested in interdisciplinary problem-solving What Participants Will Do Participants in the EarthRISE Developers Academy work in interdisciplinary teams of approximately four to five members in a fast-paced collaborative environment. Each team works on an applied research project designed to help partner organizations use NASA Earth observation data to improve workflows, planning, and management decisions. Program activities typically include: 1. Literature Review and Research Participants conduct reviews on: Relevant scientific methodologies NASA remote sensing datasets Existing applications and research approaches Scope and background of project topics 2. GIS and Remote Sensing Applications Participants use: GIS technologies Satellite imagery NASA Earth observation datasets Remote sensing tools to create practical application solutions for partner organizations. 3. Team Collaboration Participants engage in: Interdisciplinary teamwork Applied problem-solving Communication and project coordination Collaborative research development 4. Final Deliverables At the end of the program, teams complete: A final presentation for multiple audiences A technical report Project end products and application solutions Mentorship and Guidance Each project team receives support from: Project coordinators Science advisors Professional mentors NASA Langley Research Center’s EarthRISE Office This mentorship structure allows participants to gain exposure to real-world scientific workflows and professional collaboration environments. Benefits of Joining the NASA EarthRISE Developers Academy The Academy offers extensive personal, technical, and professional development opportunities. Participants can expect to strengthen skills in: Technical Skills GIS analysis Remote sensing Earth observation applications Data-driven research Scientific communication Professional Skills Presentation and public speaking Team collaboration Networking Technical writing Project development Career Development Participants also gain: Exposure to NASA projects and scientists Experience working with partner organizations Interdisciplinary research experience Insight into the geospatial workforce Professional networking opportunities The Academy also includes personality typing exercises, team-building experiences, and interactions with experts working in Earth science and applied technology sectors. Why This Opportunity Matters As global challenges related to climate, disasters, water resources, air quality, and ecological conservation continue to grow, there is increasing demand for professionals who can apply Earth observation data to real-world decision-making. NASA EarthRISE Developers Academy provides participants with a rare opportunity to: Work directly with NASA-related projects Gain applied research experience Build geospatial and Earth observation expertise Develop interdisciplinary collaboration skills Prepare for careers in the evolving Earth science and space technology sectors The program is especially valuable for individuals interested in careers involving: Climate and environmental science Geospatial technology Space applications Sustainability and resilience Remote sensing and satellite data analysis How to Apply Interested applicants should carefully follow these steps: Step 1: Read the Application Guide Review the complete application guide and eligibility requirements carefully. Step 2: Review Logistics and Project Information Explore available projects and program logistics. NASA notes that project information may continue to be updated during the application period. Step 3: Access the Application Portal Use the official EarthRISE Developers Academy Application Portal to begin and submit your application. Step 4: Prepare Supporting Materials Ensure all required application materials are completed and submitted before the deadline. Official NASA Resources Applicants are encouraged to consult the official NASA EarthRISE pages for: Application guidelines Eligibility details Project descriptions Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) Program logistics For additional inquiries, applicants may contact the NASA Langley EarthRISE Office directly through the official contact provided on the NASA website. Final Thoughts The NASA EarthRISE Developers Academy Fall 2026 represents an exceptional opportunity for students, researchers, and early-career professionals to gain hands-on experience working with NASA Earth observation data and applied science solutions. Through interdisciplinary collaboration, mentorship, and real-world research projects, participants can build valuable technical and professional skills while contributing to solutions for major global environmental challenges. For anyone interested in Earth science, GIS, remote sensing, environmental applications, or space-related innovation, this program offers a powerful pathway into the future of the geospatial and Earth observation workforce. Application Deadline: June 26, 2026 Program Dates: September 14 – November 20, 2026 Learn More and Apply Interested applicants can apply through the official application portal HERE . Click HERE to learn more about the NASA EarthRISE Developers Academy Fall 2026. Explore more global fellowship opportunities on OFY here . The post NASA EarthRISE Developers Academy Fall 2026 Applications Now Open | Join NASA’s Next Generation of Earth Observation Innovators appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Agentic AI Foundation Ambassador Program 2026: Apply to Join the First Global Cohort of Open Source AI Advocates
summary: The Agentic AI Foundation (AAIF), a Linux Foundation initiative focused on advancing open-source agentic AI technologies, has officially launched its highly anticipated AAIF Ambassador Program 2026. Applications are now open for the inaugural cohort, with submissions being accepted until June 12, 2026. This newly launched global ambassador initiative is designed for developers, educators, creators, and AI practitioners who are passionate about open-source AI and committed to helping others learn how to build with cutting-edge agentic AI technologies. The program aims to create a strong ecosystem of advocates who will educate the developer community, increase awareness of AAIF-hosted projects, and encourage broader participation in open-source AI development. What is the AAIF Ambassador Program? The AAIF Ambassador Program is an elite community initiative created by the Agentic AI Foundation to support project-based advocacy and developer education around agentic AI technologies. The first cohort will include only 10 ambassadors, making this a highly selective and prestigious opportunity for individuals actively involved in the AI and open-source ecosystem. Selected ambassadors will represent and promote AAIF projects through educational content, technical workshops, talks, tutorials, livestreams, courses, and community engagement activities. The program specifically focuses on supporting awareness, practical adoption, and contribution pathways for major AAIF-hosted projects, including: MCP Goose AGENTS.md These projects are helping shape the future of agentic AI development and collaborative open-source AI systems. Application Deadline Applications for the first AAIF Ambassador Program cohort are open until: June 12, 2026 AAIF also confirmed that future cohorts will be selected in January 2027, while applications remain open on a rolling basis throughout the year. Goals of the AAIF Ambassador Program The foundation outlined three major goals for the initiative: 1. Increase Awareness AAIF wants ambassadors to help developers understand: What agentic AI is How AAIF projects work Real-world use cases of agentic AI technologies The problems these tools solve for developers and organizations This will happen through public speaking, content creation, social media engagement, and community discussions. 2. Drive Activation The program emphasizes hands-on learning and practical developer onboarding. Ambassadors are encouraged to create resources such as: Beginner tutorials Demo projects Technical walkthroughs Livestream coding sessions Quick-start guides Educational videos The objective is to help developers experience the value of AAIF projects quickly. 3. Encourage Contribution AAIF also wants ambassadors to help developers become contributors to the open-source ecosystem. This includes: Explaining contribution pathways Hosting workshops Creating contributor guides Collaborating with maintainers Encouraging participation in the community The foundation aims to build a sustainable contributor pipeline for its growing ecosystem. Ambassador Responsibilities Every selected ambassador is expected to make: One meaningful public contribution every month Each contribution must directly relate to an AAIF project and help developers better understand, use, or contribute to the technology. Accepted Contribution Types AAIF accepts multiple forms of technical and educational contributions, including: Educational Tutorials Create step-by-step guides showing developers how to get started with AAIF projects. Conference Talks and Meetups Speak at events about how AAIF technologies fit into the future of agentic AI development. Video Content Publish short videos demonstrating project features, workflows, or use cases. Technical Blog Posts Write detailed articles explaining developer challenges and how AAIF projects solve them. Livestreams Host coding sessions, walkthroughs, or interviews with project maintainers. Online Courses Build educational courses that teach developers how to build using AAIF technologies. Benefits of Becoming an AAIF Ambassador Selected ambassadors will gain access to numerous professional, networking, and visibility benefits. Public Recognition Ambassadors will receive: Feature placement on the official AAIF Ambassador page Promotion through AAIF social channels Newsletter amplification Community-wide recognition Exclusive Insider Access Participants will gain: Access to a private Ambassador Discord community Regular briefings with AAIF leadership Direct interaction with project maintainers Early visibility into project roadmaps and updates Ambassador Swag and Credentials Selected participants will receive: Exclusive ambassador merchandise Special event credentials Official recognition materials VIP Conference Experience AAIF ambassadors may receive: Discounted conference tickets VIP seating access Lounge access during events Ambassador activities with AAIF leadership Recognition during keynote sessions Contribution Recognition System The program also includes: Points and badge systems Status titles Project-specific recognition badges Long-term community visibility Community Amplification AAIF will actively help ambassadors grow their visibility by: Sharing their content across official social platforms Featuring their work in newsletters Introducing them to project maintainers and ecosystem leaders Who Should Apply? The Agentic AI Foundation is looking for individuals who combine technical knowledge with community engagement and educational impact. Strong candidates may include: Open-source AI contributors AI engineers and developers Developer advocates Technical writers YouTubers and livestream creators Community organizers Workshop facilitators AI educators Technical bloggers Conference speakers Applicants do not need to meet every criterion, but should demonstrate a genuine interest in: Open-source AI Agentic AI systems Developer education Community growth Why This Opportunity Matters The AAIF Ambassador Program arrives at a time when interest in agentic AI systems is rapidly growing across the global technology ecosystem. As organizations increasingly adopt autonomous and collaborative AI agents, open-source infrastructure projects like MCP, Goose, and AGENTS.md are becoming increasingly important. This ambassador program offers selected individuals the opportunity to: Build global visibility Establish authority in AI and open-source communities Network with AI leaders and maintainers Gain early access to emerging technologies Contribute to the future of open-source AI infrastructure For developers and creators interested in becoming recognized voices in the AI ecosystem, this program represents a significant professional opportunity. About the Agentic AI Foundation (AAIF) The Agentic AI Foundation is a Linux Foundation initiative focused on advancing open-source technologies and ecosystems for agentic AI systems. The foundation supports collaborative development, education, and adoption of open-source AI projects that enable intelligent autonomous workflows, multi-agent systems, and developer tooling for the next generation of AI applications. How to Apply Interested applicants can submit their applications through the official AAIF Ambassador Program page. Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis, with the first cohort intake taking place in June 2026. Prospective ambassadors are encouraged to showcase: Technical expertise Educational content Community involvement Open-source contributions Experience with developer advocacy Final Thoughts The launch of the AAIF Ambassador Program marks a major step forward for the global open-source agentic AI community. By empowering educators, developers, and creators to share knowledge and build practical learning pathways, the initiative aims to accelerate the adoption and understanding of agentic AI technologies worldwide. With only 10 spots available in the inaugural cohort, competition is expected to be strong. Applicants interested in becoming early advocates of open-source agentic AI are encouraged to apply before the June 12, 2026 deadline. Learn more and apply Click HERE to learn more and apply for the Agentic AI Foundation Ambassador Program 2026. Explore more fully funded PhD opportunities on OFY HERE . The post Agentic AI Foundation Ambassador Program 2026: Apply to Join the First Global Cohort of Open Source AI Advocates appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: FAO Supporting AFR100 Programme Direct Beneficiary Grants 2026: Up to USD 50,000 for Forest and Landscape Restoration Projects in Africa
summary: The Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations (FAO), with financial support from the Government of Germany, has launched applications for the Supporting AFR100 Programme Direct Beneficiary Grants (DBG) 2026. This funding opportunity is designed to empower local organizations working to restore degraded landscapes, strengthen climate resilience, improve biodiversity, and create sustainable livelihood opportunities across selected African countries. Through this grant scheme, eligible organizations can access funding ranging from USD 5,000 to USD 50,000 to implement practical restoration initiatives that directly benefit communities and ecosystems. The initiative forms part of the broader AFR100 (African Forest Landscape Restoration Initiative), which seeks to restore millions of hectares of degraded land across Africa while supporting economic development and environmental sustainability. About the Supporting AFR100 Programme The Supporting AFR100 Programme is being implemented by the Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations (FAO) and funded by the Government of Germany. The programme aims to strengthen locally led forest and landscape restoration efforts in selected African landscapes by supporting community-based organizations and local actors that are actively involved in restoring degraded lands and promoting sustainable natural resource management. The programme is currently being implemented in: Democratic Republic of the Congo Kenya Madagascar Malawi Togo United Republic of Tanzania By supporting grassroots restoration efforts, the programme seeks to build stronger ecosystems, increase climate resilience, improve food security, and generate economic opportunities for local communities. Funding Available Under the Direct Beneficiary Grants mechanism, eligible organizations can apply for grants ranging from: Minimum Grant: USD 5,000 Maximum Grant: USD 50,000 Funding will be provided in local currency equivalent amounts. The grants are intended to support practical and measurable restoration interventions that generate positive environmental and socioeconomic outcomes. Objectives of the Direct Beneficiary Grants The Direct Beneficiary Grants scheme aims to support organizations that are: Restoring degraded lands Enhancing community resilience Improving livelihoods Promoting sustainable land management Strengthening restoration-based value chains Supporting biodiversity conservation Increasing ecosystem productivity Encouraging climate adaptation and mitigation efforts The programme recognizes that local communities play a central role in restoring landscapes and protecting natural resources. As such, the funding is designed to strengthen local ownership and leadership in restoration initiatives. Eligible Activities Organizations may seek funding for a broad range of restoration-related interventions, including but not limited to: 1. Tree Planting Initiatives Projects that promote afforestation and reforestation activities to restore degraded lands and improve ecosystem health. 2. Agroforestry Projects Activities integrating trees into agricultural systems to improve productivity, biodiversity, and climate resilience. 3. Soil and Water Conservation Initiatives focused on: Erosion control Watershed management Water harvesting Sustainable soil management practices 4. Community Nurseries Establishment and strengthening of nurseries for producing quality seedlings and supporting restoration efforts. 5. Biodiversity Conservation Projects aimed at: Protecting native species Restoring habitats Conserving ecosystems Enhancing ecological connectivity 6. Restoration-Based Livelihood Activities Income-generating activities linked to restoration efforts that support local economic development while protecting natural resources. 7. Sustainable Value Chain Development Projects that strengthen restoration-related value chains and create economic opportunities for local communities. Why This Grant Matters Across Africa, millions of hectares of land have been degraded due to unsustainable land use, deforestation, climate change, and biodiversity loss. Restoration efforts are increasingly recognized as a critical strategy for addressing these challenges. The AFR100 Programme supports locally driven solutions that can: Improve food security Increase climate resilience Restore ecosystem services Create green jobs Support sustainable agriculture Strengthen rural livelihoods Protect biodiversity Enhance water resources Reduce land degradation By investing directly in local organizations, the programme ensures that restoration solutions are community-led and adapted to local realities. Who Should Consider Applying? This opportunity is particularly suitable for: Community-based organizations Environmental NGOs Conservation organizations Agroforestry groups Farmer associations Rural development organizations Natural resource management groups Restoration-focused cooperatives Biodiversity conservation organizations Organizations with proven experience implementing restoration activities within the designated programme landscapes are especially encouraged to apply. Application Process Before beginning the application process, applicants should carefully review the official Applicant Guidelines to ensure compliance with all eligibility requirements. Organizations should ensure they: Confirm eligibility. Review the Applicant Guidelines thoroughly. Prepare all required documentation. Develop a clear and realistic project proposal. Demonstrate how proposed activities contribute to restoration and community resilience. Submit their application before the deadline. Key Benefits of the Programme Successful applicants will receive: Grant funding between USD 5,000 and USD 50,000 Support for locally led restoration initiatives Opportunity to strengthen community resilience Capacity to improve restoration-based livelihoods Contribution to national and continental restoration goals Increased environmental and social impact Enhanced ecosystem restoration outcomes Support from a globally recognized United Nations agency Important Dates Application Opening Date 22 May 2026 Application Deadline 19 June 2026 at 11:59 PM (GMT) Applicants are encouraged to submit their proposals well before the deadline to avoid last-minute technical issues. Apply Here Need More Information? Organizations seeking clarification regarding the grant opportunity can contact the programme team during the application period through: Email: afr-100-programme@fao.org Prospective applicants should consult the official Applicant Guidelines and ensure they meet all eligibility requirements before submitting their proposals. The Supporting AFR100 Programme represents an important opportunity for local organizations across Africa to access funding that can help restore degraded landscapes, strengthen livelihoods, and contribute to a more resilient and sustainable future. For more information on this grant opportunity click here Discover more grant opportunities on OFY, visit here The post FAO Supporting AFR100 Programme Direct Beneficiary Grants 2026: Up to USD 50,000 for Forest and Landscape Restoration Projects in Africa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Howard Hughes Medical Institute (HHMI) Presidential Fellow 2026–2027: Earn Up to $200,000 While Working Directly with the President of One of the World’s Leading Science Organizations
summary: The Howard Hughes Medical Institute (HHMI) has opened applications for its highly competitive Presidential Fellow Program , an exceptional opportunity designed for outstanding early-career professionals who want to work at the highest levels of leadership, strategy, innovation, and scientific philanthropy. Based at HHMI Headquarters in Chevy Chase, Maryland, USA , the fellowship places a small cohort of only 2–3 fellows per year directly within the Office of the President. Unlike traditional fellowships that focus primarily on observation or rotations, HHMI Presidential Fellows are entrusted with leading strategic projects, engaging with senior executives, and helping shape the future direction of one of the world s most influential scientific organizations. With a salary ranging from $150,000 to $200,000 annually , this fellowship offers an unparalleled opportunity for ambitious young professionals interested in science, technology, artificial intelligence, policy, philanthropy, education, communications, and organizational leadership. About Howard Hughes Medical Institute (HHMI) Howard Hughes Medical Institute is one of the world s largest private funders of biomedical research. The organization supports: Scientists at leading universities Research institutes Hospitals Science education initiatives Film and media projects Artificial intelligence-enabled research programs HHMI is widely recognized for making long-term investments in talented individuals whose work has the potential to transform science and society. Its headquarters, located in Maryland near Washington, D.C., houses experts from numerous disciplines, including: Biomedical sciences Investments Communications Human resources Technology Digital production Operations Strategic planning Through these efforts, HHMI contributes to groundbreaking discoveries and educational initiatives that impact people around the world. What is the HHMI Presidential Fellow Program? The Presidential Fellows Program is a prestigious leadership fellowship that places selected fellows directly in the Office of the President. Fellows work under the guidance of: The President of HHMI The Vice President Chief Operating Officer (COO) Senior Advisor to the President The fellowship is modeled after the Special Assistant to the President role in government, providing participants with high-level exposure, responsibility, and influence. Rather than observing projects from the sidelines, fellows actively lead strategic initiatives and contribute directly to organizational decision-making. Why This Fellowship Stands Out Very few fellowships offer young professionals the opportunity to operate at the intersection of: Science Artificial Intelligence Philanthropy Education Media Technology Institutional Strategy Participants gain direct access to senior leadership while developing skills that are highly transferable across academia, government, nonprofit organizations, consulting firms, and the private sector. What Presidential Fellows Will Do Selected fellows will take ownership of important organizational projects and initiatives. Key responsibilities include: 1. Lead Cross-Functional Strategic Projects Fellows will manage projects across multiple departments, including: Science programs Artificial intelligence initiatives Finance Human resources Communications Technology Science education Film and media 2. Drive Strategic Decision-Making Participants will: Analyze complex issues Scope challenges Develop strategic options Coordinate stakeholders Support executive decision-making Their role is to move initiatives from concept toward implementation. 3. Manage AI-Powered Workflows A unique feature of this fellowship is its focus on artificial intelligence. Fellows will: Utilize AI agents Coordinate Large Language Model (LLM) workflows Accelerate institutional projects through emerging technologies This makes the fellowship particularly attractive to candidates interested in AI and innovation. 4. Handle Sensitive Executive-Level Projects Fellows will frequently work on confidential matters requiring: Professional judgment Discretion Strategic thinking Executive communication 5. Pursue Self-Directed Projects One of the most attractive aspects of the program is the opportunity to dedicate: 10% of work time to approved self-directed projects aligned with the fellow’s interests and professional goals. Who Should Apply? HHMI seeks intellectually curious individuals who can quickly learn across disciplines and thrive in fast-paced environments. The fellowship welcomes both: Outstanding recent graduates Candidates with advanced degrees Educational Requirements Applicants must possess: Required Bachelor s degree Preferred but Not Required Advanced degrees such as: MBA MPP PhD JD Equivalent graduate qualifications Experience Requirements Applicants should have: 0–5 years of professional experience Relevant backgrounds may include: Consulting Public policy Research Strategy Law Operations Technology Management Analytics Skills and Qualities HHMI Is Looking For Successful candidates should demonstrate: Strong Analytical Ability The ability to understand unfamiliar topics quickly and translate information into actionable recommendations. AI Fluency Applicants should have experience using: Artificial intelligence tools Large Language Models (LLMs) AI-assisted workflows AI agents Exceptional Communication Skills Candidates must be able to: Write clearly Present complex ideas simply Communicate effectively with senior leaders Adaptability The role requires comfort with: Ambiguity Rapidly changing priorities Executive-level environments Intellectual Curiosity HHMI values candidates who are interested in multiple fields, including: Science Technology Public policy Education Organizational leadership Fellowship Duration The fellowship is a: Fixed-Term Position 2–3 years This extended duration allows fellows to contribute meaningfully to institutional priorities and gain significant leadership experience. Location The fellowship is based at: HHMI Headquarters Chevy Chase The role is primarily on-site. Salary and Compensation One of the most attractive aspects of the fellowship is its highly competitive compensation package. Annual Salary USD $150,000 – $200,000 Additional benefits include: Health coverage Retirement plans Paid leave Wellness programs Employee recognition initiatives Benefits of the HHMI Presidential Fellowship Selected fellows will gain: Professional Benefits Executive-level exposure Direct mentorship from senior leaders Strategic leadership experience Cross-sector project management experience Career Benefits Expanded professional network Strong portfolio of high-impact projects Enhanced leadership credentials Increased career opportunities across sectors Personal Development Benefits Advanced problem-solving skills AI leadership experience Strategic thinking capabilities Organizational governance exposure Diversity and Inclusion HHMI is committed to fostering an inclusive workplace and welcomes applications from individuals of diverse backgrounds. The organization values: Diversity of thought Inclusive leadership Equal opportunity Collaborative work environments HHMI is an Equal Opportunity Employer. How to Apply Interested candidates should submit their application through the HHMI Careers Portal. Applications are currently open. Official Career Portal HHMI Careers Page Applicants are encouraged to prepare a strong application highlighting: Leadership experiences Strategic projects AI-related skills Analytical abilities Communication strengths Apply Here Why You Should Consider This Fellowship The HHMI Presidential Fellows Program is one of the rare opportunities that places early-career professionals directly at the center of leadership within a globally respected scientific institution. For individuals interested in shaping the future of science, technology, philanthropy, and artificial intelligence while developing world-class leadership skills, this fellowship offers an exceptional platform for growth and impact. For more information on this fellowship visit here .
